Bash · Build · Go
Operating systems for small businesses

Your apps don't need to be replaced.
They need to talk to each other.

I build harnesses — systems that pull together the apps you already pay for (your POS, scheduler, accounting, comms, project tools) into one operational view, with logic that fits how your business actually runs.

Bash · Build · Go
01

The apps are good. They just don't talk.

Square, Toast, Mindbody, QuickBooks, Procore, Calendly. These tools work. The problem is they don't know about each other. So you become the integration layer — copying numbers between dashboards, exporting CSVs, reconciling at month-end, answering the same question in three places because none of them know what the others know.

02

The harness is what was missing.

What I build is the connective tissue. Every app you use stays in place. The harness pulls their data into one operational view, layers rules on top, and exposes everything through a dashboard plus a Telegram agent. Your team works one way. The apps keep running their own way. Nothing gets replaced.

03

Rules first. AI where it earns its keep.

This is not an AI product. The foundation is hard-coded logic, plain integrations, JSON, code that works the same way every time. AI shows up as a layer — the Telegram agent, plain-English questions, summaries when they're useful. It does not run your business in the background, and it does not bill you per token to do basic things.

Every business runs differently.
So every harness is built differently.

Patterns repeat — yoga studios, salons, restaurants, retail floors, and GC operations all share a shape. So the harnesses ship with templates. But no two operators run their shop the same way, and no template survives contact with a real business. Every deployment is its own build.

Some are wider — multi-location, more channels, more integrations. Some are deeper — heavy customization for an operator with strong opinions about how their floor runs. All of them start from the harness.

The work

Two kinds of work. One operating philosophy.

OpenDev keeps complex deals aligned while they move. The harnesses keep operating businesses coordinated while the day is happening. Different problems, same operating philosophy.

Contract architecture · Live

OpenDev

Smart legal contract methodology for commercial development.

Build-to-suit developments, complex construction deals, multi-party contracts where four or five parties have to coordinate over a year or more. OpenDev classifies every operative term in every contract, routes changes through defined procedures, and keeps the live deal state visible to every seat in real time.

Not every seat is a party, not every action binds, and every approval has a defined route before the dispute happens.

Full methodology and demo live at OpenDev.Studio: a fifteen-minute interactive negotiation walkthrough and a field memo on how the system keeps the contract live.

Explore OpenDev.Studio →
Custom harnesses

Built for the way each operator runs.

Five demos below. Each is a real harness for a real shape of business — anonymized, but operational. Your name and email get you the access code for all of them.

A harness is not a replacement app. It is the operating layer that connects the apps you already use, applies your business rules, and gives the operator one place to see what is happening.

Counter

For small brick-and-mortar.

A configurable harness for shops with one revenue line. Tattoo studio, hair salon, yoga & fitness, restaurant & bar, boutique retail. Pick a persona, plug in your apps, run your day from one view.

Square · Toast · Mindbody · Booksy · Resy · Shopify POS · QuickBooks
Try the demo →
Inkwell Studio

Services + retail + F&B, in one place.

A deeper build for studios that combine appointments, walk-in retail, and an espresso or wine bar. Today board, artist payroll, inventory, messages, finance — all coordinated. The hybrid bot handles common questions; live AI for the rest.

Square · Mindbody · QuickBooks · Stripe
Try the demo →
Fitness, Recovery & Beverage Spa

One studio, three revenue lines.

The deepest brick-and-mortar harness — built for an operator running fitness classes, recovery services, and a beverage program under one roof. Coverage tracking, manager load, equipment maintenance, today's focus. No bot here, by design — the dashboard does the work.

Mindbody · Square · Toast · QuickBooks
Try the demo →
Apex Build Group · GC harness

For GCs done living in spreadsheets.

A financial command view for general contractors. Pulls cost data, schedules, and actuals from the tools you already use and surfaces job-by-job profit and loss in real time. Live dashboard, job management, estimate builder, AI project agent.

Procore · Sage · Buildertrend · Bluebeam · QuickBooks
Try the demo →
Best Cleaning Service Ever

Multi-city services, one operator.

For a services business operating across more than one market. Booking, dispatch, team coordination, payments, customer comms — unified across cities. Comes with a scripted bot tour guide so first-time visitors get walked through the dashboard.

Square · QuickBooks · Twilio
Try the demo →

Operator's ventures

Three businesses I run on these systems.

Proof of concept, in the most direct sense. I don't just build harnesses for hypothetical operators. I run small businesses myself, and the systems that keep them coordinated are the ones I sell.

Small moves and handyman services in greater Memphis. Booking, deposits, scheduling, and field ops all run through one harness.
